advert_voting_details.prompt:
You are analyzing a 3-second video clip (75 frames at 25.0fps) showing the
FINAL FRAMES OF AN ADVERTISEMENT for Disneyland paris and the first few frames of the next advertisement.

## ADVERT INFORMATION
- Brand: Disneyland paris
- Advertiser: Walt disney company
- Category: Overseas resorts
- Duration: 20 seconds seconds

## YOUR TASK

Identify the EXACT LAST FRAME where Disneyland paris appears in this clip
- Look carefully at all frames, looking for Disneyland paris logos and visual brand identifiers or textual information featuring Walt disney company or Disneyland paris
- Use the brand, advertiser, category and duration information - Disneyland paris, Walt disney company, Overseas resorts, 20 seconds seconds - to help identify the precise end of the advert for Disneyland paris
- The last few frames will usually show a logo for the brand, or a product design. Pay close attention to logos / visual branding for Disneyland paris - the advert always ends with logos, text or other visual brand identifiers
- The product image is not always the end of the advert - branding text, logos or brand identifiers are most likely to end the advert - so look for Disneyland paris identifiers in final frames even if the product is shown before
- If you see a series of frames with visual brand identifiers for Disneyland paris, then a different advert / product appears, the very last frame with Disneyland paris is the one to select

## OUTPUT FORMAT

Return EXACTLY this XML structure:

<advert>
    <last_frame>FRAME_NUMBER</last_frame>
    <confidence>HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW</confidence>
    <description>Brief reason for decision</description>
</advert>

## NOTES

- Frame 0 is the first frame of the video (1.5s BEFORE the estimated end of the advert for Disneyland paris)
- Frame 74 is the last frame of the video (1.5s AFTER the estimated end of the advert for Disneyland paris)
- The estimated end of the 'Disneyland paris' advert is at frame 37 but your job is to identify the actual end frame
- Return a frame integer (between 0-74), not a timecode

- Brand: Google pixel 8 phone

- Category: Mobile handsets - smartphones
- Duration: 20 seconds seconds

Identify the EXACT LAST FRAME where Google pixel 8 phone appears in this clip
- Look carefully at all frames, looking for Google pixel 8 phone logos and visual brand identifiers or textual information featuring Google uk or Google pixel 8 phone
- Use the brand, advertiser, category and duration information - Google pixel 8 phone, Google uk, Mobile handsets - smartphones, 20 seconds seconds - to help identify the precise end of the advert for Google pixel 8 phone
- The last few frames will usually show a logo for the brand, or a product design. Pay close attention to logos / visual branding for Google pixel 8 phone - the advert always ends with logos, text or other visual brand identifiers
- The product image is not always the end of the advert - branding text, logos or brand identifiers are most likely to end the advert - so look for Google pixel 8 phone identifiers in final frames even if the product is shown before
- If you see a series of frames with visual brand identifiers for Google pixel 8 phone, then a different advert / product appears, the very last frame with Google pixel 8 phone is the one to select

## OUTPUT FORMAT

Return EXACTLY this XML structure:

<advert>
    <last_frame>FRAME_NUMBER</last_frame>
    <confidence>HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW</confidence>
    <description>Brief reason for decision</description>
</advert>

## NOTES

- Frame 0 is the first frame of the video (1.5s BEFORE the estimated end of the advert for Google pixel 8 phone)
- Frame 74 is the last frame of the video (1.5s AFTER the estimated end of the advert for Google pixel 8 phone)
- The estimated end of the 'Google pixel 8 phone' advert is at frame 37 but your job is to identify the actual end frame
- Return a frame integer (between 0-74), not a timecode

FINAL FRAMES OF AN ADVERTISEMENT for Domestos power foam and the first few frames of the next advertisement.

## ADVERT INFORMATION
- Brand: Domestos power foam
- Advertiser: Unilever uk home & p
- Category: Toilet cleaners/fresheners
- Duration: 20 seconds seconds

## YOUR TASK

Identify the EXACT LAST FRAME where Domestos power foam appears in this clip
- Look carefully at all frames, looking for Domestos power foam logos and visual brand identifiers or textual information featuring Unilever uk home & p or Domestos power foam
- Use the brand, advertiser, category and duration information - Domestos power foam, Unilever uk home & p, Toilet cleaners/fresheners, 20 seconds seconds - to help identify the precise end of the advert for Domestos power foam
- The last few frames will usually show a logo for the brand, or a product design. Pay close attention to logos / visual branding for Domestos power foam - the advert always ends with logos, text or other visual brand identifiers
- The product image is not always the end of the advert - branding text, logos or brand identifiers are most likely to end the advert - so look for Domestos power foam identifiers in final frames even if the product is shown before
- If you see a series of frames with visual brand identifiers for Domestos power foam, then a different advert / product appears, the very last frame with Domestos power foam is the one to select

## OUTPUT FORMAT

Return EXACTLY this XML structure:

<advert>
    <last_frame>FRAME_NUMBER</last_frame>
    <confidence>HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW</confidence>
    <description>Brief reason for decision</description>
</advert>

## NOTES

- Frame 0 is the first frame of the video (1.5s BEFORE the estimated end of the advert for Domestos power foam)
- Frame 74 is the last frame of the video (1.5s AFTER the estimated end of the advert for Domestos power foam)
- The estimated end of the 'Domestos power foam' advert is at frame 37 but your job is to identify the actual end frame
- Return a frame integer (between 0-74), not a timecode

advert_voting_details.prompt:
You are analyzing a 3-second video clip (75 frames at 25.0fps) showing the
FINAL FRAMES OF AN ADVERTISEMENT for Lidl easter and the first few frames of the next advertisement.

## ADVERT INFORMATION
- Brand: Lidl easter
- Advertiser: Lidl uk gmbh
- Category: Food & drink - multi product
- Duration: 20 seconds seconds

## YOUR TASK

Identify the EXACT LAST FRAME where Lidl easter appears in this clip
- Look carefully at all frames, looking for Lidl easter logos and visual brand identifiers or textual information featuring Lidl uk gmbh or Lidl easter
- Use the brand, advertiser, category and duration information - Lidl easter, Lidl uk gmbh, Food & drink - multi product, 20 seconds seconds - to help identify the precise end of the advert for Lidl easter
- The last few frames will usually show a logo for the brand, or a product design. Pay close attention to logos / visual branding for Lidl easter - the advert always ends with logos, text or other visual brand identifiers
- The product image is not always the end of the advert - branding text, logos or brand identifiers are most likely to end the advert - so look for Lidl easter identifiers in final frames even if the product is shown before
- If you see a series of frames with visual brand identifiers for Lidl easter, then a different advert / product appears, the very last frame with Lidl easter is the one to select

## OUTPUT FORMAT

Return EXACTLY this XML structure:

<advert>
    <last_frame>FRAME_NUMBER</last_frame>
    <confidence>HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW</confidence>
    <description>Brief reason for decision</description>
</advert>

## NOTES

- Frame 0 is the first frame of the video (1.5s BEFORE the estimated end of the advert for Lidl easter)
- Frame 74 is the last frame of the video (1.5s AFTER the estimated end of the advert for Lidl easter)
- The estimated end of the 'Lidl easter' advert is at frame 37 but your job is to identify the actual end frame
- Return a frame integer (between 0-74), not a timecode

advert_voting_details.prompt:
You are analyzing a 3-second video clip (75 frames at 25.0fps) showing the
FINAL FRAMES OF AN ADVERTISEMENT for Insure & go travel insurance and the first few frames of the next advertisement.

## ADVERT INFORMATION
- Brand: Insure & go travel insurance
- Advertiser: Insure & go
- Category: Travel & sport insurance
- Duration: 30 seconds seconds

## YOUR TASK

Identify the EXACT LAST FRAME where Insure & go travel insurance appears in this clip
- Look carefully at all frames, looking for Insure & go travel insurance logos and visual brand identifiers or textual information featuring Insure & go or Insure & go travel insurance
- Use the brand, advertiser, category and duration information - Insure & go travel insurance, Insure & go, Travel & sport insurance, 30 seconds seconds - to help identify the precise end of the advert for Insure & go travel insurance
- The last few frames will usually show a logo for the brand, or a product design. Pay close attention to logos / visual branding for Insure & go travel insurance - the advert always ends with logos, text or other visual brand identifiers
- The product image is not always the end of the advert - branding text, logos or brand identifiers are most likely to end the advert - so look for Insure & go travel insurance identifiers in final frames even if the product is shown before
- If you see a series of frames with visual brand identifiers for Insure & go travel insurance, then a different advert / product appears, the very last frame with Insure & go travel insurance is the one to select

## OUTPUT FORMAT

Return EXACTLY this XML structure:

<advert>
    <last_frame>FRAME_NUMBER</last_frame>
    <confidence>HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW</confidence>
    <description>Brief reason for decision</description>
</advert>

## NOTES

- Frame 0 is the first frame of the video (1.5s BEFORE the estimated end of the advert for Insure & go travel insurance)
- Frame 74 is the last frame of the video (1.5s AFTER the estimated end of the advert for Insure & go travel insurance)
- The estimated end of the 'Insure & go travel insurance' advert is at frame 37 but your job is to identify the actual end frame
- Return a frame integer (between 0-74), not a timecode

advert_voting_details.prompt:
You are analyzing a 3-second video clip (75 frames at 25.0fps) showing the
FINAL FRAMES OF AN ADVERTISEMENT for Age uk weekly lottery and the first few frames of the next advertisement.

## ADVERT INFORMATION
- Brand: Age uk weekly lottery
- Advertiser: Age uk
- Category: Lottery
- Duration: 60 seconds seconds

## YOUR TASK

Identify the EXACT LAST FRAME where Age uk weekly lottery appears in this clip
- Look carefully at all frames, looking for Age uk weekly lottery logos and visual brand identifiers or textual information featuring Age uk or Age uk weekly lottery
- Use the brand, advertiser, category and duration information - Age uk weekly lottery, Age uk, Lottery, 60 seconds seconds - to help identify the precise end of the advert for Age uk weekly lottery
- The last few frames will usually show a logo for the brand, or a product design. Pay close attention to logos / visual branding for Age uk weekly lottery - the advert always ends with logos, text or other visual brand identifiers
- The product image is not always the end of the advert - branding text, logos or brand identifiers are most likely to end the advert - so look for Age uk weekly lottery identifiers in final frames even if the product is shown before
- If you see a series of frames with visual brand identifiers for Age uk weekly lottery, then a different advert / product appears, the very last frame with Age uk weekly lottery is the one to select

## OUTPUT FORMAT

Return EXACTLY this XML structure:

<advert>
    <last_frame>FRAME_NUMBER</last_frame>
    <confidence>HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW</confidence>
    <description>Brief reason for decision</description>
</advert>

## NOTES

- Frame 0 is the first frame of the video (1.5s BEFORE the estimated end of the advert for Age uk weekly lottery)
- Frame 74 is the last frame of the video (1.5s AFTER the estimated end of the advert for Age uk weekly lottery)
- The estimated end of the 'Age uk weekly lottery' advert is at frame 37 but your job is to identify the actual end frame
- Return a frame integer (between 0-74), not a timecode

FINAL FRAMES OF AN ADVERTISEMENT for Consorzio grana padano cheese and the first few frames of the next advertisement.

- Brand: Consorzio grana padano cheese
- Advertiser: Consorzio tutela gra
- Category: Cheese - continental
- Duration: 10 seconds seconds

Identify the EXACT LAST FRAME where Consorzio grana padano cheese appears in this clip
- Look carefully at all frames, looking for Consorzio grana padano cheese logos and visual brand identifiers or textual information featuring Consorzio tutela gra or Consorzio grana padano cheese
- Use the brand, advertiser, category and duration information - Consorzio grana padano cheese, Consorzio tutela gra, Cheese - continental, 10 seconds seconds - to help identify the precise end of the advert for Consorzio grana padano cheese
- The last few frames will usually show a logo for the brand, or a product design. Pay close attention to logos / visual branding for Consorzio grana padano cheese - the advert always ends with logos, text or other visual brand identifiers
- The product image is not always the end of the advert - branding text, logos or brand identifiers are most likely to end the advert - so look for Consorzio grana padano cheese identifiers in final frames even if the product is shown before
- If you see a series of frames with visual brand identifiers for Consorzio grana padano cheese, then a different advert / product appears, the very last frame with Consorzio grana padano cheese is the one to select

## OUTPUT FORMAT

Return EXACTLY this XML structure:

<advert>
    <last_frame>FRAME_NUMBER</last_frame>
    <confidence>HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW</confidence>

</advert>

## NOTES

- Frame 0 is the first frame of the video (1.5s BEFORE the estimated end of the advert for Consorzio grana padano cheese)
- Frame 74 is the last frame of the video (1.5s AFTER the estimated end of the advert for Consorzio grana padano cheese)
- The estimated end of the 'Consorzio grana padano cheese' advert is at frame 37 but your job is to identify the actual end frame
- Return a frame integer (between 0-74), not a timecode

advert_voting_details.prompt:
You are analyzing a 3-second video clip (75 frames at 25.0fps) showing the
FINAL FRAMES OF AN ADVERTISEMENT for Fast cash property and the first few frames of the next advertisement.

## ADVERT INFORMATION
- Brand: Fast cash property
- Advertiser: Fastcashproperty
- Category: General property services
- Duration: unknown seconds

## YOUR TASK

Identify the EXACT LAST FRAME where Fast cash property appears in this clip
- Look carefully at all frames, looking for Fast cash property logos and visual brand identifiers or textual information featuring Fastcashproperty or Fast cash property
- Use the brand, advertiser, category and duration information - Fast cash property, Fastcashproperty, General property services, unknown seconds - to help identify the precise end of the advert for Fast cash property
- The last few frames will usually show a logo for the brand, or a product design. Pay close attention to logos / visual branding for Fast cash property - the advert always ends with logos, text or other visual brand identifiers
- The product image is not always the end of the advert - branding text, logos or brand identifiers are most likely to end the advert - so look for Fast cash property identifiers in final frames even if the product is shown before
- If you see a series of frames with visual brand identifiers for Fast cash property, then a different advert / product appears, the very last frame with Fast cash property is the one to select

## OUTPUT FORMAT

Return EXACTLY this XML structure:

<advert>
    <last_frame>FRAME_NUMBER</last_frame>
    <confidence>HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW</confidence>
    <description>Brief reason for decision</description>
</advert>

## NOTES

- Frame 0 is the first frame of the video (1.5s BEFORE the estimated end of the advert for Fast cash property)
- Frame 74 is the last frame of the video (1.5s AFTER the estimated end of the advert for Fast cash property)
- The estimated end of the 'Fast cash property' advert is at frame 37 but your job is to identify the actual end frame
- Return a frame integer (between 0-74), not a timecode

[THINKING]
The user wants me to identify the last frame of the "Fast cash property" advertisement.

1.  **Analyze the video:**
    *   **00:00 - 00:16:** The video shows a static image or slow animation of houses with text overlay: "FAST CASH PROPERTY", "Open 24/7", and a phone number "0800 085 13 66". This is clearly the advertisement for Fast Cash Property.
    *   **00:17 - 00:20:** A transition occurs. Blue blocks slide in to form the number "1111".
